description: This figure presents the Differential Thermal Analysis (DTA) thermogram of 35 h milled Fe₄₀Mn₁₉Ni₁₅Al₁₅Si₁₀C₁ high entropy steel powder, tested in the temperature range of 200 °C to 1000 °C. The y-axis denotes heat flow in μV/mg, with the downward direction indicating exothermic events. Two distinct exothermic peaks are clearly observed on the curve, located at 420 °C and 520 °C respectively. The exothermic event at ~420 °C corresponds to the ordering and/or texturing of the γ-brass type phase, while the peak at ~520 °C is attributed to the formation of Fe₅Si₃-type intermetallic phase. The thermogram demonstrates that the 35 h milled powder is thermally stable up to 500 °C, and phase transformations occur above this temperature, providing direct thermal behavior evidence for the phase evolution analysis of the alloy during subsequent annealing and sintering processes.
